mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent signals persistent labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand recovery, which sustains earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative or flat when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession expansion phase reflects improving capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 70% of weeks where INDPRO increases by more than 0.5% during the recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of central bank policy tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor demand that supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative when PAYEMS shows positive growth for three consecutive months in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with stable consumer prices creates a favorable macro environment that boosts corporate margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative when GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.0% and CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5% in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals an immediate supply-side contraction that disrupts manufacturing output and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of weeks where INDPRO declines by more than 0.3% month-over-month.
